text.title: PHP Dual Gate: PHPStan Level 9 + Psalm Taint Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: phpstan.neon MUST set `level: 9` (or `level: max` for libraries); strict types enabled.
rule.rationale: Level 9 forces strict null + array shapes; lower levels miss the bug classes the methodology targets.

rule.statement: psalm.xml MUST enable `--taint-analysis` (or equivalent <taintAnalysis enabled="true"/>) for every PHP repo with user input.
rule.rationale: Taint catches SQLi / XSS / command injection that type checks alone miss.

rule.statement: CI MUST run BOTH analyzers as separate jobs; failure of either fails the PR.
rule.rationale: Different bug classes; merging output hides one tool's findings under the other's noise.

rule.statement: Existing violations are captured via phpstan-baseline.neon / psalm-baseline.xml; new violations on PR fail. Baseline shrinks over time, never grows.
rule.rationale: Incremental adoption: baseline lets the gate go green immediately, then ratchets.

rule.statement: @phpstan-ignore-next-line / @psalm-suppress comments MUST include a justification on the same line.
rule.rationale: Silent suppressions accumulate; named justifications make them reviewable.

rule.statement: If PHP < 7.4 OR codebase relies heavily on magic methods / dynamic props OR repo throwaway, STOP.
rule.rationale: False-positive flood on dynamic PHP overwhelms the gate.
